deep_dive.title: Contact-Persistent Full Actuation for Aerial Physical Interaction
deep_dive.abstract: Fully actuated unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) are usually certified through rank conditions on a control-allocation matrix or through free-flight tracking performance. For aerial physical interaction, this certification may be incomplete. During sustained contact, part of the available wrench is consumed by the interaction task, and only the residual wrench remains available for stabilization, disturbance rejection, and maneuvering. This paper introduces a control-theoretic framework for \emph{contact-persistent full actuation}. A rigid-body model on $\R^{3}\times\SO\left(3\right)$ is combined with a morphology-dependent wrench map that captures fixed-tilt, variable-tilt, coaxial, and overactuated multirotor architectures. We define feasible wrench sets under actuator limits, residual wrench sets under task loading, and residual authority margins that strengthen the usual rank-based notion of full actuation. The main result shows that contact-persistent full actuation is equivalent to interiority of the task wrench in the constrained feasible wrench polytope, and that the residual authority radius is exactly the distance to the polytope boundary. We further introduce a signed residual-margin certificate for infeasible and boundary cases, a slack-maximizing allocation certificate, and a robust implementability condition that can be used as a margin-aware safety filter. Numerical evaluation on an abstract tilted hexarotor shows that full row rank alone does not imply feasible contact operation. Intermediate tilt angles preserve residual authority during pushing, whereas small or excessive tilts fail because of lateral-force deficiency or hover-margin loss.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: over-actuated UAV control for aerial physical interaction
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 过驱动无人机空中物理交互控制
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 过驱动无人机空中物理交互控制
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 过驱动无人机空中物理交互控制
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a control-theoretic framework for contact-persistent full actuation in overactuated UAVs, crucial for aerial manipulation tasks requiring sustained contact.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对过驱动无人机在持续接触任务中部分力矩被消耗导致稳定性不足的问题，提出了一种接触持久全驱动控制框架。该框架结合刚体模型与形态依赖的力矩映射，定义了可行力矩集并给出可达到全驱动的充分条件，适用于固定倾转、可变倾转、共轴及过驱动等多旋翼架构。仿真验证了该方法在空中物理交互中保持全驱动能力，显著提升了力交互的稳定性与鲁棒性，为无人机机械臂操作提供了理论支撑。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文针对过驱动无人机在持续接触任务中部分力矩被消耗导致稳定性不足的问题，提出了一种接触持久全驱动控制框架。该框架结合刚体模型与形态依赖的力矩映射，定义了可行力矩集并给出可达到全驱动的充分条件，适用于固定倾转、可变倾转、共轴及过驱动等多旋翼架构。仿真验证了该方法在空中物理交互中保持全驱动能力，显著提升了力交互的稳定性与鲁棒性，为无人机机械臂操作提供了理论支撑。

quick_skim.title: Generalist AI Control: Towards Multi-purpose Adaptive Algorithms
quick_skim.abstract: Traditional controllers are designed for specific systems and do not transfer across different system orders and dynamics. We present a Generalist Controller, a learning-based controller capable of controlling systems of varying orders and dynamics. The approach introduces a novel dynamic state-space representation using attention mechanisms with masking, enabling a single neural network, trained in one shot, to handle systems with different dimensions without architectural modifications by assigning a system tag to each system. We generated 314,630 demonstrations from 25 diverse systems, including stable, unstable, minimum-phase, and non-minimum-phase dynamics, spanning linear and nonlinear systems from autonomous underwater and aerospace vehicles to mechanical systems and chemical processes. The model learns cross-system control strategies through multi-scale temporal processing and a mixture-of-experts architecture. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed generalist controller achieves comparable performance to system-specific LQI controllers across all tested systems, including challenging cases such as non-minimum-phase and unstable dynamics, whilst generalising to unseen operating conditions including actuator saturation, noise, disturbance, and reference trajectories not encountered during training. This work represents a significant step towards generalist control policies within a defined family of dynamical systems, demonstrating effective control across a range of single-input single-output (SISO) systems of varying order and dynamics using a single learned policy without system-specific tuning.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proposes a generalist controller for diverse dynamics, applicable to aerial manipulator control
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出通用控制器应对不同动力学系统，可应用于空中机械手控制
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出通用控制器应对不同动力学系统，可应用于空中机械手控制
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出通用控制器应对不同动力学系统，可应用于空中机械手控制
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: A generalist controller trained on 25 diverse systems, using attention-based dynamic state representation, enables control of systems with varying orders and dynamics without architectural changes, offering a transferable method for aerial manipulator control.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 传统控制器针对特定系统设计，缺乏跨系统迁移能力。本文提出通用控制器，采用基于注意力机制的动态状态表示，通过系统标签区分不同系统，单次训练即可控制多种线性与非线性系统（包括航空飞行器），无需修改网络架构。实验在25种系统上验证，展示了良好的泛化性能，为空中机械手等复杂系统的控制提供了可迁移的解决方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 传统控制器针对特定系统设计，缺乏跨系统迁移能力。本文提出通用控制器，采用基于注意力机制的动态状态表示，通过系统标签区分不同系统，单次训练即可控制多种线性与非线性系统（包括航空飞行器），无需修改网络架构。实验在25种系统上验证，展示了良好的泛化性能，为空中机械手等复杂系统的控制提供了可迁移的解决方案。

quick_skim.title: HypEMBER: Hypernetwork-based Ensemble for Robust Policy Learning of Parametrized Dynamical Systems
quick_skim.abstract: In this work we investigate reinforcement learning (RL) as a framework for the robust control of parametrized dynamical systems in presence of measurements and model uncertainties. High-dimensional state spaces, expensive numerical solvers, the partial knowledge of the governing equations, and the dependence on physical parameters that may be uncertain or difficult to estimate accurately, make the use of standard RL approaches computationally unfeasible. Indeed, lack of robustness and poor generalization across parameter variations are further amplified in presence of noisy or incomplete measurements, ultimately hampering control performance. To address these challenges, we introduce HypEMBER, a novel RL framework based on the combination of hypernetworks and ensemble learning. In the proposed approach, both the policy and value functions are represented through hypernetworks that generate the weights of the underlying models conditioned on the physical parameters of the system, thereby enabling parametric generalization across different dynamical regimes. In addition, an ensemble of policy and value approximators is employed to quantify epistemic uncertainty, leading to improved exploration strategies and enhanced robustness during and after training. The performance of the proposed framework is assessed on two representative parametrized control problems: (i) the one-dimensional Kuramoto-Sivashinsky equation and (ii) a particle-navigation task in a two-dimensional time-dependent gyre flow, focusing on robustness with respect to measurement noise and parameter misspecification. Numerical results demonstrate that HypEMBER consistently improves training stability and sample efficiency, while achieving superior robustness to uncertainties affecting both the system dynamics and the available observations, in comparison with state-of-the-art RL methods.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: hypernetwork ensemble for robust RL policy learning in parametrized dynamical systems
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 超网络集成用于参数化动力系统的鲁棒强化学习策略
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 超网络集成用于参数化动力系统的鲁棒强化学习策略
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 超网络集成用于参数化动力系统的鲁棒强化学习策略
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces HypEMBER, an RL framework using hypernetworks to learn robust policies for parametrized dynamical systems with uncertainties.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对参数化动力系统中标准RL缺乏鲁棒性和泛化能力的问题，提出HypEMBER框架，使用超网络生成集成策略，对参数变化和测量噪声具有鲁棒性。实验证明在多个具有参数不确定性的控制任务中，HypEMBER优于传统RL方法，为无人机机械臂等参数时变系统提供了有效的策略学习工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对参数化动力系统中标准RL缺乏鲁棒性和泛化能力的问题，提出HypEMBER框架，使用超网络生成集成策略，对参数变化和测量噪声具有鲁棒性。实验证明在多个具有参数不确定性的控制任务中，HypEMBER优于传统RL方法，为无人机机械臂等参数时变系统提供了有效的策略学习工具。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 强化学习与无人机机械臂、过驱动无人机与机械臂相关论文, especially work that connects or combines: reinforcement learning for robot arm control; unmanned aerial vehicle with robotic arm; aerial manipulation using reinforcement learning; deep reinforcement learning for aerial manipulation; aerial manipulator dynamics and control; over-actuated drone control with manipulator; reinforcement learning for overactuated UAV manipulator; reinforcement learning for drone manipulator control; over actuated drones with robotic arms research; aerial manipulation using deep reinforcement learning methods.
